column6: Line jammed
column13:
1. Primary Cause: The broken lever of the counterweight resulted in a blockage in the discharge zone of the FK Pump.
2.Secondary Cause: Excessive moisture in the coal caused material to become sticky, leading to frequent jamming of the screw feeder and pipeline choke.
column14: 1.Replacement of Broken Counterweight Lever:Replace the damaged lever with a new one of the same specification. Ensure proper alignment, welding (if required), and balancing during installation Pre-Drying of Material.

2.Clearing of Jammed Material:Thoroughly clean the FK pump discharge zone and surrounding areas to remove all moist/sticky buildup.Ensure material handling routes (conveyors, hoppers, silos) are protected from rain exposure.
3.Monitoring and Logging- Begin a daily inspection log during rainy season to record any unusual noises, lever movements, or material buildup

column6: B-Phase Cable Lugs & Bolt Overheating For Which ID Fan-1 Was Stopped For Preventive Maintenance
column9: GI Nut Bolts Replaced to SS Nut Bolts, 280mm Cooling Fan Installed
column13:
As The ID Fan & Chimney Area is High Sulphur(So2) Emitting Area.
The Sulphur Becomes Sulphuric Acid (H2So4) In Contact With Air & Moisture.
These Sulphuric Acid Gets Composited on Copper Bus Bar, SS Nut, Bolt, Washer, GI Lugs & Cable Tray Causing the Outer Layers Fade & Exposing the Internal Iron Metal.
The Whole Phenomenon Caused Performance Losses.
column14:
For Excess Heat Control We Have Installed a Bigger Panel Cooling Fan From 140mm to 280mm For More Efficient Panel Cooling, Now Temp is at 51°c.
GI Type Nut, Bolt & Washers Replaced by SS Type Which Are Better in H2So4 Resistant.
Also, There is Proposed Plan to Shift the ID Fan Local JB to a Safer Location to Avoid Such Conditions.

column6:
C-17 Conveyor ZSS Problem problem occurred.
Due to dust accumulation this problem happened.
column9:
1. A protective canopy was installed over the ZSS sensor and controller to shield them from conveyor dust spillage, preventing dust accumulation and ensuring reliable sensor performance.
2. Also JH team were suggested to increase cleaning schedule.
column13:
The C-17 Conveyor ZSS Problem occurred because dust spillage from the conveyor accumulated on the sensor sensing area, obstructing its ability to detect conveyor movement accurately. Investigation revealed that inadequate spillage control and insufficient housekeeping around the conveyor allowed dust to build up over time.
As a result, the sensor generated an incorrect signal, leading to the Problem. To prevent recurrence, dust-spillage control, regular housekeeping, sensor-area inspections, and protective shielding for the sensor be implemented.
column14: 1. A protective canopy was installed over the ZSS sensor and controller to shield them from conveyor dust spillage, preventing dust accumulation and ensuring reliable sensor performance.
2. Also JH team were suggested to increase cleaning schedule.

column6: PGP Breaker / Tripped on Ground Fault
column9:
Replaced the damaged section of the power cable with proper high-voltage splicing/jointing kit, test insulation resistance (Megger), and reset the PGP breaker.
Power cable was rerouted from the route of duct line work to prevent any further hazard.
column13:
he PGP Breaker damage occurred because a severe ground fault problem current rushed back into the system when the incoming power cable from Pellet-1 was punctured. 
Investigation revealed that CMT duct line laying work was executed directly over the cable route work.
column14:
